The plant is native to Africa and is also known as the "twisted succulent" due to its unique and twisted stems. The Fockea comaru is a type of succulent that stores water in its stems, allowing it to survive in dry environments. The plant is relatively unknown outside of its native region, but it has gained popularity among plant enthusiasts and collectors.
